rule: hit iff kw>=2 OR sem>=0.7 OR func>=0.7

results.title: Moonrise spawning timing PNAS
results.rationale: source-domain biology paper; no LLM mechanism overlap

results.title: External-Clock Synchronous Multi-Agent Broadcast (2026/3)

results.title: Barrier-Synchronized Multi-Agent Consensus (2025/10)

results.title: Free-MAD Consensus-Free MAD
results.rationale: parallel responses without chained debate captures simultaneous broadcast structure

rationale_summary: Ranks 6 and 7 are direct functional duplicates of REEF-SPAWN's external-clock barrier-synchronized broadcast aggregation (func ~0.93 and ~0.90). Ranks 2, 3, 5, and 8 are strong structural matches (func 0.70-0.80). Seven of eight results clear the hit threshold, confirming the mechanism is well-covered in 2023-2026 multi-agent LLM literature.
report: Re-judged all 8 results against REEF-SPAWN (external-clock-triggered barrier-synchronized broadcast aggregation across N agents) using the supplied calibration anchors. Result: 7 hits, 1 miss, identical to 07_hit_miss.json. Rank 1 (PNAS coral biology) is the only miss (sem ~0.10, func ~0.05). Rank 6 (arxiv 2603.04205) and rank 7 (arxiv 2510.18820) are direct functional duplicates at func ~0.93 and ~0.90 respectively. Ranks 2 (S2-MAD), 3 (Adaptive Stability MAD), 5 (MAD baseline), and 8 (Free-MAD) clear both sem>=0.7 and func>=0.7 thresholds. Rank 4 (CONSENSAGENT) clears only sem>=0.7 (func 0.65). No kw-forced hits because the candidate's content_words are mostly source-domain coral biology terms or composite phrases that do not lexically appear in LLM paper titles/snippets. Verdict: duplicate; the mechanism is well-covered by existing barrier-synchronized multi-agent consensus work.
